summary: Rough audience tape that still captures an emotional Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude and a standout Built to Last performance.
review: The recording is an audience source with generational copies, giving it a fairly lo‑fi but listenable sound. Musically the show is uneven – the first set drifts, while the second delivers highlights like Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude, Built to Last and a tight Cold Rain > Minglewood segue – with Jerry’s voice noted as being in good shape. Fans’ reactions range from high enthusiasm to disappointment, making the overall impression mixed but leaning positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise several songs despite noting a lo‑fi audience source; the weighted blend of decent musical moments (70%) with fair recording quality (30%) yields a solid above‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Rough audience tape, multi‑generation copies; occasional hollowness and lo‑fi character but captures the emotional peaks of the performance.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as sleepy and unfocused; the second set lifts off with Built to Last and several tight segues, delivering a mixed‑but‑memorable overall flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His voice is praised as being in good shape and his tone/emotion on Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude noted as outstanding.
